steps.title: Keep one main verb
steps.content: The sentence already has the main verb phrase "might yield." The blank is inside the noun phrase about a New Historicist analysis of historical context, so it must modify "analysis" rather than create another main verb.

steps.title: Reject finite verb phrases
steps.content: A, B, and C are finite verb phrases, so each would create two main verbs in one clause. A present participle can modify "analysis" while leaving "might yield" as the main verb. The answer is D.
